{"verdict":"request_changes","summary":"The new declarations are in a plausible algebraic-group file, but one import is evidently broader than what the file uses.","findings":[{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean","line":9,"issue":"`Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure` is imported even though the file only uses the `IsAlgClosed` class and never uses `AlgebraicClosure` or algebraic-closure constructions.","fix":"Replace it with the direct module that provides `IsAlgClosed`, or remove it if another existing import already provides the class transitively.","evidence":"Diff lines 9 and 44: the import is `Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure`, while the only related use is the hypothesis `[IsAlgClosed k]`."}]}

verdict_obj.summary: The new declarations are in a plausible algebraic-group file, but one import is evidently broader than what the file uses.

findings.issue: `Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure` is imported even though the file only uses the `IsAlgClosed` class and never uses `AlgebraicClosure` or algebraic-closure constructions.
findings.fix: Replace it with the direct module that provides `IsAlgClosed`, or remove it if another existing import already provides the class transitively.
findings.evidence: Diff lines 9 and 44: the import is `Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure`, while the only related use is the hypothesis `[IsAlgClosed k]`.
